instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `fluid-let` that temporarily assigns new values to a list of special variables for the duration of body, restoring the originals via unwind-protect. Use gensyms to save the prior values.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro fluid-let (bindings &body body)
  (let ((saved (mapcar (lambda (b) (declare (ignore b)) (gensym)) bindings)))
    `(let ,(mapcar (lambda (s b) (list s (car b))) saved bindings)
       (unwind-protect
            (progn ,@(mapcar (lambda (b) `(setf ,(car b) ,(cadr b))) bindings)
                   ,@body)
         ,@(mapcar (lambda (s b) `(setf ,(car b) ,s)) saved bindings)))))
original_failure_log_excerpt:

            (UNWIND-PROTECT (PROGN (SETF *DEBUG* T) (RUN)) (SETF *DEBUG* #:G1)))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 *DEBUG*))
            (UNWIND-PROTECT (PROGN (SETF *DEBUG* T) (RUN))
              (SETF *DEBUG* #:G112)))
